title: Dependency Resolution
description: Resolving version constraints, solving dependency graphs, and managing the lock file in Composer

use_cases.name: Run Install
use_cases.description: Developer triggers install; if no lock file exists, automatically falls back to update

use_cases.name: Run Update (Resolve & Lock)
use_cases.description: Full dependency resolution: builds pool, solves constraints, writes lock file

use_cases.name: Solve Dependency Graph
use_cases.description: SAT-based solver resolves all package version constraints into a LockTransaction

use_cases.name: Write Lock File
use_cases.description: Persists resolved packages, platform reqs, aliases, and stability flags to composer.lock

use_cases.name: Read Locked Repository
use_cases.description: Loads locked packages from composer.lock into a LockArrayRepository for install/verify

use_cases.name: Verify Lock File Freshness
use_cases.description: Checks content-hash of composer.json against lock file; warns if stale

use_cases.name: Check Lock File Existence
use_cases.description: Checks whether a valid composer.lock with packages exists

use_cases.name: Install from Lock File
use_cases.description: Installs packages based on locked versions; verifies lock is installable on current platform

use_cases.name: Extract Non-Dev Packages
use_cases.description: Runs second solver pass to determine which packages are non-dev only

use_cases.name: Validate Missing Requirements
use_cases.description: Compares lock file packages against root requires to detect missing/incompatible constraints

use_cases.name: Select Preferred Packages
use_cases.description: Policy selects best candidate from decision queue (prefer-stable, prefer-lowest, etc.)

use_cases.name: Generate Content Hash
use_cases.description: Computes MD5 of relevant composer.json keys (require, require-dev, repositories, etc.) for freshness checks

steps: runSat() → propagate() / revert() / analyze() on conflicts
doUpdate → extractDevPackages() [second Solver pass, non-dev only]
doUpdate → Locker::setLockData(packages, devPackages, platformReqs, ...) → writes composer.lock
If not locked → falls back to doUpdate path
doInstall → Solver::solve() to verify platform compatibility

key_relationships: Installer depends_on Solver (creates new Solver instance in doUpdate/extractDevPackages)
Installer depends_on Locker (reads/writes lock file, checks freshness)
Solver depends_on Pool (package lookup via pool::whatProvides())
Solver depends_on PolicyInterface (package selection via selectPreferredPackages())
Solver depends_on RuleSetGenerator (rule generation in solve())
Solver depends_on RuleWatchGraph + Decisions (SAT propagation internals)
Solver returns LockTransaction (consumed by Installer for lock file writing and install)
Locker uses ArrayLoader (deserializes lock data to Package objects)
Locker uses ArrayDumper (serializes Package objects for lock file)

LockArrayRepository.description: In-memory repository populated from lock file data; holds locked package versions

Pool.description: Aggregates all candidate packages from registered repositories; supports whatProvides() lookups

important_notes: Solver uses SAT (Boolean satisfiability) algorithm with CDCL-style conflict learning
Conflicts produce SolverProblemsException with human-readable problem descriptions
Lock file freshness is based on content-hash (MD5 of relevant composer.json keys)
Two solver passes occur during update: one for all packages, one for non-dev-only classification
doInstall can be called standalone (fresh install from lock) or chained after doUpdate
Partial updates (updateAllowList) require an existing lock file
Platform requirements (PHP version, extensions) are filtered via PlatformRequirementFilterInterface
